reasoning: The current baseline value of 1-year expected inflation is likely 2.689 (the August 2025 data point) or 2.789 (the July 2025 data point). Over the past 13 months, the mean value of this series was 2.52, with a median of 2.62. Both potential baselines are above average, placing them in the upper quartile of recent observations. While the linear trend over the last year is slightly upward, inflation expectations tend to exhibit mean-reverting behavior around the long-term target, and the Month-over-Month changes have been highly volatile with negative autocorrelation. Due to this mean reversion, future observations are more likely to fall back toward the ~2.5% to 2.6% average rather than exceed the high baseline. Therefore, the probability of seeing an increase above 2.689 (or 2.789) is below 50%. Following standard forecasting principles, uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, pulling the probabilities for the later dates (November 2025 and February 2026) progressively closer to 0.50.
